Ad Text:
This is an exciting opportunity for an Automation Engineer to contribute to Automation projects within the Energy and Utilities industry. Based in Perth, you will play a pivotal role in delivering high-quality automation solutions.

Client Details

This organisation is a medium-sized company known for its innovative approach to engineering and manufacturing. They are dedicated to delivering exceptional solutions to meet industry demands while maintaining a strong commitment to quality and excellence.

Description

Design, develop, and implement automation systems to streamline production processes
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to ensure seamless project delivery.
Develop and optimise control systems to enhance operational efficiency.
Conduct system testing and troubleshooting to ensure functionality and compliance.
Provide technical support and training to internal teams as required.
Prepare technical documentation, including system specifications and user manuals.
Stay updated on the latest trends and technologies in automation and healthcare engineering.
Ensure all projects adhere to industry standards and regulatory requirements.

Profile

A successful Automation Engineer should have:

A degree in engineering, automation, or a related field.
Experience designing and implementing automation systems in a manufacturing setting.
Strong problem-solving skills and attention to detail.
Knowledge of control systems, PLC programming, and SCADA systems.
Familiarity with healthcare industry standards and regulations.
Excellent communication and teamwork abilities.

Job Offer

12-month contract with long-term potential.
Opportunities to work on cutting-edge projects in the healthcare industry.
Supportive and collaborative work environment in Perth.
Exposure to the latest technologies and industry practices.

If you are an experienced Automation Engineer looking to make a significant impact in the Utilities and Mining sector, we encourage you to apply today!

Staff Data Engineer
Melbourne (1 Day a week onsite)
Up to $185,000 + Super + Profit Share

The Opportunity
Our client is one of Australia's most recognised global product houses, operating at scale across multiple international markets. Their data engineering team sits at the core of the business, building and maintaining the platform that powers every product decision, every customer interaction, and every commercial outcome the organisation makes.

You'll be joining as the most senior data engineering voice in the Melbourne team. This isn't a role where you're handed a backlog and told to ship tickets. You'll be shaping the technical direction of the platform, mentoring the engineers around you, and working directly with senior stakeholders to translate business problems into scalable data solutions.

AI is central to how this team operates day-to-day, not something on the roadmap.

What You'll Be Doing
Leading and evolving a modern data platform built on BigQuery, Snowflake, Airflow, and dbt at scale and in production. Driving architectural decisions that span the full data lifecycle including ingestion, transformation, orchestration, and delivery. Mentoring and developing engineers across a distributed team with a genuine focus on raising the standard of the people around you.  Building and operationalising AI and ML pipelines hands-on, not from the sidelines. Partnering directly with senior business stakeholders to translate commercial requirements into technical solutions. Contributing to company-wide data strategy through the internal Tech Lead Forum.

What We're Looking For

Demonstrated experience at Staff or Tech Lead level in a data engineering environment where you've operated beyond your immediate team
Hands-on production experience with Apache Airflow at platform ownership level, not just DAG writing
Strong cloud platform experience with GCP preferred and AWS considered
A genuine mentorship track record where you've developed junior and mid-level engineers in a meaningful and sustained way
Practical AI and ML pipeline experience building and operationalising pipelines around models


Helpful, But Not Essential

dbt expertise including model materialisation strategies and cost-aware design
CDP platform experience with RudderStack, Segment, or equivalent
Experience facilitating agile sprints and owning project delivery end to end
Familiarity with martech stacks including CDPs, ad platforms, and analytics tooling

Why Join DNX Solutions?

From day one at DNX, you’ll feel part of a strong, supportive team where collaboration and growth are at the heart of everything we do. You’ll never feel alone—there’s always someone ready to help, guide, and share knowledge. DNX Solutions has earned the 2025 Great Place to Work® Certification! It is the gold standard for workplace excellence worldwide, awarded based on direct feedback from employees, measuring trust, workplace culture, and job satisfaction. Earning this certification highlights our commitment to fostering an environment where our team members feel valued, supported, and empowered to excel.

At DNX, your career path is clear, and your learning never stops. We invest in both your professional and personal growth, ensuring you develop new skills, stay ahead in the industry, and build a career that excites you.

And let’s be honest—we’re a cool bunch of people! We love what we do, and we bring that passion into our work and team culture. If you're looking for a place where you’ll be challenged, supported, and surrounded by a fun and driven team, DNX is the place for you

Ad Text:

If you live in Australia or New Zealand, you've likely heard of AMP. But at a time when society is changing, we are too. We're now a nimbler business with new leadership and thinking.

For us, these are exciting times. There's a real potential for big thinkers to help us redefine what financial services could be. And turn our legacy into something even more positive and powerful for the future.

Help people create their tomorrow, while you create yours

We help people with their banking, super, retirement and finances. Through upturns, downturns, recessions, and major life transitions. Every day, we help people see and make more of their financial potential, so that they can create their tomorrow. And we've been doing it for over 170 years.

If we do our job well, we genuinely add to the prosperity of our country and its people.

How you'll make an impact


Engineer, operate, and continuously improve enterprise‑scale data platforms and data pipelines on AWS, enabling secure, scalable, and highly reliable analytics and data products across the organisation.

Design and build end‑to‑end data pipelines across the full SDLC, translating business and analytical requirements into robust technical designs, development, testing, deployment, and ongoing optimisation.

Take end‑to‑end ownership of deliverables with minimal supervision, proactively identifying risks, clarifying requirements, and driving work through to production outcomes.

Develop high‑performance big data processing solutions using Apache Spark (extensive Scala/Spark expertise is required; PySpark also desirable), Python, and SQL, optimising for large‑scale batch and streaming workloads.

Build, maintain, and optimise data pipelines using the AWS data stack, including Amazon S3, AWS Glue, EMR, Athena, and Amazon Redshift, ensuring performance, scalability, and cost efficiency.

Apply AI‑driven software engineering practices to accelerate delivery and improve quality, including AI‑assisted code generation, refactoring, automated testing, documentation, and operational troubleshooting across data pipelines.

Apply AI‑driven design techniques to data platform and pipeline architecture, leveraging AI tools to evaluate architectural options, optimise data flows, improve schema and partition design, and enhance performance and cost outcomes.

Design and automate batch and streaming data pipelines, implementing resilient orchestration, dependency management, and error‑handling patterns aligned with enterprise standards and best practices.

Define and operate CI/CD and automation practices for data pipelines and infrastructure, including Git‑based version control, automated testing, deployment pipelines, and infrastructure‑as‑code, with a strong focus on repeatability and reliability.

Establish and enforce data governance controls across the AWS data ecosystem, including data access management, schema evolution, data quality frameworks, metadata management, and data lifecycle and retention policies.

Implement secure data integration patterns, including IAM‑based access controls, secrets management, encryption at rest and in transit, and secure connectivity between AWS services and upstream and downstream systems.

Embed observability and reliability practices into data pipelines, including structured logging, metrics, data quality monitoring, alerting, runbooks, and operational dashboards to enable proactive issue detection and resolution.

Support incident and problem management for data platforms and pipelines, performing root cause analysis, implementing remediation actions, and driving continuous improvement informed by operational insights and AI‑assisted analysis.

Maintain visibility of enterprise data assets, including datasets, tables, pipelines, jobs, models, and usage patterns, and proactively identify and remediate data quality issues, pipeline failures, or unmanaged assets.

Communicate clearly and work collaboratively with engineering, analytics, architecture, security, risk, and business stakeholders, translating requirements into delivery plans and ensuring alignment on scope, trade‑offs, and outcomes.

Partner with security, risk, and compliance teams to embed data security, privacy, and regulatory controls into data platform designs and pipeline implementations from design through to production.

Implement compliance and data protection controls, including data classification, retention, auditing, and access reviews, in line with enterprise policies and regulatory obligations.


What you'll bring to the role

Strong experience engineering, operating, and scaling enterprise data platforms on AWS or comparable cloud ecosystems, with a focus on reliability, security, performance, and operational excellence.

Extensive experience with big data platforms and distributed data processing, with deep, hands‑on knowledge of Scala and Apache Spark (design, performance tuning, and production operations) across large‑scale datasets.

Hands‑on experience designing and building end‑to‑end data pipelines, including batch and streaming workloads, using AWS services such as Amazon S3, AWS Glue, EMR, Athena, Redshift, and associated orchestration frameworks.

Practical experience applying AI‑assisted software engineering techniques across the full data pipeline SDLC, including AI‑driven design, code generation, refactoring, automated testing, optimisation, documentation, and operational troubleshooting.

Experience designing data models, curated data layers, and data products that support analytics, reporting, and downstream consumption at scale.

Strong experience implementing data governance, security, and compliance controls across cloud data platforms, including data access management, encryption at rest and in transit, data classification, retention, and auditing.

Proven experience defining and operating CI/CD pipelines for data engineering workloads, including environment management, release automation, and infrastructure‑as‑code using tools such as GitHub Actions or equivalent CI/CD platforms.

Solid understanding of identity and access management in cloud environments, including IAM‑based access control, role separation, secrets management, and secure service‑to‑service integration.

Experience implementing monitoring, observability, and operational reliability practices for production data platforms and pipelines, including logging, metrics, alerting, data quality monitoring, and incident management.

Demonstrated ability to operate and support enterprise‑grade data platforms, including incident response, root cause analysis, performance tuning, and continuous improvement.

Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to explain complex technical concepts to different audiences and collaborate effectively within a team environment.

Highly self‑organised and delivery‑focused, with a demonstrated ability to work autonomously end‑to‑end (from problem definition through build, release, and production support), proactively unblocking yourself and minimising dependency on others while collaborating effectively when needed.

Exposure to responsible data and AI practices, including data ethics, model risk awareness, data usage controls, and governance frameworks applied within analytics and data platform environments.

Experience working with enterprise data governance tooling and practices, such as data cataloguing, metadata management, data lineage, data quality frameworks, and policy enforcement across cloud data platforms.

Relevant cloud or data engineering certifications, preferably in AWS (Data Analytics, DevOps, or Solutions Architecture) or comparable cloud data and DevOps certifications.

Strong collaboration and stakeholder engagement skills, with the ability to work effectively in enablement‑driven and governance‑led environments involving security, risk, compliance, architecture, and analytics stakeholders.

Experience designing and implementing AI‑augmented data patterns, including vector storage, embeddings, semantic search, and knowledge grounding techniques to support advanced analytics, search, or downstream AI use cases.

Familiarity with agentic and workflow‑driven design patterns as applied to data engineering, including multi‑step pipeline orchestration, tool integration, automated decision logic, and guardrails embedded into data processing workflows.


You'll thrive here if…

If you can adapt from BAU to the ambiguous with ease, you'll do well here. Change is never easy, so bring your commitment, grit and growth mindset.

Because we run lean, you'll be expected to jump in and deliver across a variety of areas. Meaning, you'll be closer to the action and executive decisions that influence where we go next.

If you're someone that can hold their own, you'll find AMP quite liberating.

Why we think you'll love working at AMP

Doing what we've always done is not an option, so your clever ideas will get airtime here. You'll be encouraged to speak up and try new things. If they don't work, we move on - better for it.

We know there's no one way of doing things. So, you won't have to sacrifice who you are or how you work to fit in here. We're inclusive and flexible in many of the ways you'd expect. And in some of the ways you wouldn't. As long as your health and wellbeing come first - at home and at work.

In fact, most of what makes AMP such a welcoming, enjoyable place to work are our people. Wherever you go, you'll find moments to connect, feel valued and do meaningful work.

Whether it's through our first-class leaders who are invested in you and your success. Through year-round opportunities to volunteer, fundraise and give back to the community. Or in the everyday challenges you face as we work together to strengthen this great organisation. Challenges that will stretch you, amplify your potential and compound the impact you have.



We believe in the power of inclusion and diversity

We're dedicated to fostering inclusion, diversity, and a warm feeling of belonging at AMP. It sparks creativity, ignites innovation, and turns up the dial on the quality of our decisions and performance. This not only makes our workplace more engaged, but also leads to better connections with our customers.

We're your allies in the search for the perfect fit - when you apply, let us know how we can support you to put your best self forward during our selection process.

We're also committed to enhancing employment opportunities for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people, so we enthusiastically encourage candidates from these backgrounds to apply and explore our Reconciliation Plan on our website

Ready to create your tomorrow?

If you're someone who sees opportunity where others see challenge, come and work with us in smart, progressive ways as we transform an iconic Australian brand for the future. And, through a series of career-defining moments, create your own tomorrow.

Don't procrastinate! We review applications when we get them, and if we discover the ideal candidate, we may close the role earlier than the advertised close date.

Looking forward to meeting you.

About the role

As a Mechatronic Field Service Engineer at Automation & Robotic Technologies, you will play a vital role in providing expert technical support and maintenance services for our cutting-edge industrial automation and robotic systems across Victoria, and other States in Australia. This full-time position is based in our Bayswater office, with the opportunity to travel to customer sites as needed.

What you'll be doing

Conduct on-site diagnostics, troubleshooting and repair of complex mechatronic systems and equipment

Perform scheduled preventative maintenance and servicing to ensure optimal performance and reliability

Liaise with customers to understand requirements, provide technical advice and recommendations

Document all work completed and maintain detailed records of service history

Support the implementation of new automation equipment and provide training to customer operators and technicians

Work closely with the engineering team to identify opportunities for system improvements

What we're looking for

Degree in Mechatronic, Electrical or Electronic Engineering, or a relevant technical discipline

Minimum 3-5 years' experience as a Mechatronic Field Service Engineer or similar role

Proven track record of fault-finding, diagnostics and repair of industrial automation equipment

Strong working knowledge of PLC, HMI, drives and control systems programming

Excellent problem-solving, communication and customer service skills

Ability to work independently and as part of a team to deliver exceptional service

Valid driver's license and willingness to travel to customer sites as required

What we offer

At Automation & Robotic Technologies, we are committed to providing a supportive and rewarding work environment for our team. In addition to a competitive salary, you will enjoy access to ongoing training and development opportunities, a collaborative and innovative culture, and excellent benefits .

About us

Automation & Robotic Technologies is a leading provider of advanced industrial automation solutions and robotic systems. With over 20 years of experience, we partner with some of the biggest names in automation to design, install and maintain cutting-edge equipment and technologies that drive efficiency, productivity and safety. Our passionate team of engineers and technicians are at the forefront of the industry, constantly innovating to push the boundaries of what's possible.

If you're ready to take the next step in your career as a Mechatronic Field Service Engineer, we'd love to hear from you so Apply now to become part of our dynamic team.

Job Title: Computer Systems Architect - Basic Wings Course
Salary: Buy Additional Annual Leave + many more benefits
Ad Text:
About us:

At Lockheed Martin Australia, we believe that when people are empowered, inspired, and supported, they can achieve extraordinary things. We embrace flexibility, so our people can be their best inside and outside of work. We champion inclusion and continuous learning, ensuring all voices are heard and valued. And we thrive on collaboration, turning challenges into opportunities for growth and innovation.  As a leader in national security, our innovative and diverse programs create a more secure future for Australia across space, air, land, sea, and cyber domains. Every day is a chance to solve the world’s most complex challenges and make a profound impact. Experience it here.

The Basic Wings Course (BWC) Computer Systems Architect will be the primary on-site engineering point of contact for the BWC program, providing engineering support and coordination across the various ICT and Ground Based Training System (GBTS) Configuration Item (CI)’s in accordance with Lockheed Martin Australia’s internal processes and program specific objectives.

Responsibilities

·         The Basic Wings Course Computer Systems Architect is required to:

·         Be the primary on-site engineering point of contact for the program management office, in service support team, development teams, subcontractors and the customer.

·         Co-ordinate/conduct engineering investigations for the ICT and Ground Based Training System (GBTS) elements, including troubleshooting and documenting HW and SW issues.

·         Co-ordinate/conduct engineering on-site integration and Verification and Validation activities for all ICT and Ground Based Training System (GBTS) tech insertions and major changes.

·         Co-ordinate/support trade studies to select fit for purpose components and technologies.

·         Support the development and implementation of ICT and GBTS changes in accordance with Engineering Change Management processes.

·         Co-ordinate/support the development and delivery of Installation Data Pack (IDP) documentation.

·         Support the development and delivery of ICT and GBTS Verification & Validation (V&V) documentation.

·         Support the development and delivery of computer systems Bill of Material (BOM) documentation.

 

About you:

To be successful in this role you will also have:

Extensive experience in installation/deployment, configuration and verification of ICT Hardware and Software;

·         Extensive experience conducting Engineering Investigations (troubleshooting, change impact etc) for ICT Hardware/Software

·         Extensive experience in the development of computer system infrastructure.

·         Excellent analysis skills with an ability to analyse and develop alternatives to determine best value based on customer driven objectives.

·         Excellent communication and collaboration skills to effectively work with cross-functional teams, stakeholders, and vendors.

·         Tertiary qualifications from an accredited institution in a related discipline (typically Information Technology), and/or equivalent experience/combined education. Certifications like CCNA, CCNP, CCDE, Network+, RHCE, MCSAetc. are highly regarded.

·         Demonstrated ability to guide and mentor small teams of computer systems architects.

Desirable

·         Experience with simulation devices including simulation environment software (MS Flight sim, Prepar3d etc) and simulation peripherals (simulator controls, VR headsets, projectors etc)

·         Experience working in Software Development programs;

·         Demonstrated experience working within an engineering or project environment (preferably in the defence industry).

·         Demonstrated experience working in a dispersed project team environment.

Ad Text:
At Lockheed Martin Australia, we believe that when people are empowered, inspired, and supported, they can achieve extraordinary things. We embrace flexibility, so our people can be their best inside and outside of work. We champion inclusion and continuous learning, ensuring all voices are heard and valued. About the program
AIR6500 is the RAAF’s $3 billion program to develop a joint air battle management system that builds on the capabilities offered by Next Generation sensor and command and control systems. AIR6500 will create a fully networked ‘system of systems’ that synchronises air and missile defence operations in joint and coalition environments, maximising situational awareness and enhancing the speed of decision making across the battlespace.

Your responsibilities will include:

The AIR6500 Phase 1 Enabling ICT Team Lead is required to:

• Lead the planning, estimating and communication of the Support System ICT development lifecycles.

• Lead the day-to-day planning and tasking for the AIR6500 Phase 1 Support System ICT Team.

• Lead the lifecycle of AIR6500 Phase 1 Support System functional & non-functional computer system requirements.

• Lead the development and delivery of AIR6500 Phase 1 Support System preliminary and detailed computer system designs.

• Lead trade studies to select fit for purpose computer systems components and technologies.

• Lead the development and delivery of computer systems Bill of Material (BOM) documentation.

• Lead the development and delivery of computer systems Installation Data Pack (IDP) documentation.

• Lead the development and delivery of computer systems Verification & Validation (V&V) documentation.

• Lead all changes to the AIR6500 Phase 1 Support System in accordance with Engineering Change Management.

 

About you:

Extensive experience in the development of computer system infrastructure.

• Excellent analysis skills with an ability to analyse and develop alternatives to determine best value based on customer driven objectives.

• Excellent communication and collaboration skills to effectively work with cross-functional teams, stakeholders, and vendors.

• Tertiary qualifications from an accredited institution in a related discipline (typically Information Technology), and/or equivalent experience/combined education. Certifications like CCNA, CCNP, CCDE, Network+, RHCE etc. are highly regarded.

About m-View Live Video

m-View Live Video provides mobile video solutions, including live streaming, body-worn and in-car systems, all managed through a digital evidence platform. We support law enforcement, public safety, government, and private sectors with reliable, high-quality tools that improve safety and decision-making.

About the role

We’re growing and seeking an experienced Linux Developer to join our Embedded & Multimedia team in a full-time, permanent role based in Melbourne (hybrid: 3 days in office). You’ll develop software for IoT devices, delivering secure, end-to-end video solutions from device to cloud, with a strong focus on evidence integrity for law enforcement use.

Your profile

You enjoy building Linux-based device software and integrating it into secure, large-scale systems. You’ll develop and maintain C/C++ (plus Go/Rust) applications on embedded Linux, focusing on device agents, telemetry, secure data handling, and compliance. The role covers the full lifecycle—from design to deployment—working closely with backend and QA teams.

You’ll also handle device provisioning, identity, telemetry, and reliable data transfer across intermittent networks. Experience with video or AI is helpful but not essential; strong Linux systems and secure device knowledge are key.

Essential skills

Strong C/C++ and familiarity with Go/Rust

Embedded Linux development experience

Networking (TCP/IP, HTTP, device-to-cloud)

Multithreading and long-running services

Secure device communication and provisioning

Experience with telemetry, reliability, and intermittent networks

Linux tools, debugging, and scripting (Python/shell)

Agile teamwork

QUIC/HTTP3, OTA updates, large-scale IoT systems

Edge AI, multimedia streaming (RTSP, WebRTC, etc.)

Security (secure boot, encryption, key storage)

Video tools (GStreamer, FFmpeg) and standards

Networking, databases, containers, CI/CD

Troubleshooting and performance optimisation

Why Apply?

m-View offers career development, flexible hybrid work, training support, social events, and a modern CBD office. This is a high-impact role shaping mobile video technology, requiring ownership, quality, and accountability.

Requirements

Full Australian working rights

Ability to pass a National Police Check

Immediate start available

Melbourne is Australia's fastest growing city, and public transport is vital to keeping it moving. Since 2009, Metro has proudly operated and maintained a world-class train service and delivered major transport projects that shape the future of our city. Guided by our value of Safely Always, we are one team dedicated to ensuring everyone gets home safely while helping to connect and create a better Melbourne.

At Metro, we are one team, united in our purpose of moving Melbourne.


We believe in fostering an environment where your skills, talents, and aspirations can thrive. We look forward to supporting you as you plan the next chapter of your career at Metro.





About the Role

This role helps shape and safeguard the systems and data that keep Melbourne's train network running. You'll be a trusted technical expert, helping the organisation understand and adopt integration best practices, solve complex problems at the intersection of enterprise IT and operational technology, and ensure that the integrations you own are reliable, secure, and built to last. You will work across a modern cloud integration platform (Boomi, Solace, AWS and Azure) as part of a team that takes pride in the quality and resilience of what it delivers.

The successful candidate will bring proven experience designing and developing integrations using iPaaS (preferably Boomi) and cloud integration technologies, with an ability to translate complex technical concepts into clear, accessible language for a range of business stakeholders. They will be comfortable guiding the organisation's understanding of integration architecture and best practices.

This is a full‑time, permanent role based at 700 Collins Street, Docklands, with hybrid working arrangements available.

Key Responsibilities:

Design, configure, build, test, deploy and manage integration solutions based on Solace and Boomi in cloud hosted environment.
Take accountability of end-to-end integration solution from source to destination and collaborate effectively with architects and other system owners to ensure a stable, secure, and reliable integration is delivered and maintained.
Apply Integration best practices by ensuring adherence to defined integration platform patterns / processes and design standards.
Document technical designs and solutions.
Support Integration platform lead in analysing, estimating, and costing new requirements and with prioritisation and planning of team's activities and platform roadmap.
Liaise with business and technical stakeholders/teams to understand requirements, resolve issues and dependencies and support project managers with scheduling, costing, and tracking risks, issues, and progress.
Contribute to the IT Service Management of Boomi Solace platform, and the APIs/Integrations deployed on the platforms which includes 24/7 on-call rosters, incident management, problem management, monitoring and routine and reactive maintenance ensuring minimum disruption to business operations.
Liaise with support services and product vendors to ensure adequate levels of monitoring and ongoing support of the integration processes, including ongoing software releases, patching and software upgrades for the optimal running of the integration platforms.
Ensure SLA adherence both through internal support and vendor management against committed SLA.

Qualifications, Skills, and Experience:

Tertiary studies in a Software Engineering/ Computer Science or equivalent disciplines.
Experience working in Azure or AWS cloud environments.
Experience with scripting languages (e.g. Python, PowerShell, Bash) and API design and testing tools (OpenAPI/Swagger, Postman)
Experience building tools or automations that connect systems or data sources, whether that's Excel with Power Query or macros, SharePoint workflows, scripts or any solution where moving data from one place to another
Understanding of how MTMs operational and business systems fit together. You know how data flows between systems, where pain points are and why getting the data right matters for the network.
Worked with structured data formats or protocols e.g. pulling data from APIs, working with CSV/XML/JSON exports, automating file transfers or querying databases
Comfortable communicating technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders. You have translated between business needs and technical solutions, or written documentation on technical solutions that is in-use.

FREE Myki card for PTV travel across Victoria
14 weeks Paid Parental Leave (no qualifying period)

Diversity & Inclusion - we value unique perspectives and support initiatives like Women at Metro (over 30% of our workforce are women)

If this role resonates with your values, experience, and career growth, then please apply.

About the Company

We’re partnering with a highly capitalised, ambitious business building a completely new category: AI-native services platforms. Instead of selling software into companies, they acquire and own real-world service businesses and rebuild them with AI embedded at the core of operations.

The ambition is to transform industries that have historically been impossible to scale due to reliance on human judgement. By embedding AI directly into decision-making, operations and workflows, these businesses become compounding, software-like platforms rather than traditional services businesses.

The long-term vision is to create a platform where intelligence compounds across multiple industries, improving with every business, every system and every decision.

About the Role

As a AI Full Stack Engineer, you will be part of the small, high-calibre team building the core intelligence layer that powers this model.

This is not a feature-focused role. You will be designing and deploying systems that sit at the heart of real operating businesses, directly influencing how work gets done and how decisions are made.

You will:

Design and build end-to-end AI-native systems across frontend, backend and infrastructure
Develop LLM-powered applications including agents, orchestration layers and decision systems
Build internal tools and interfaces used daily by operators across multiple industries
Create data and feedback loops that allow systems to continuously improve
Work directly with real-world problems that are often ambiguous, messy and high impact
The output of your work will not sit in a dashboard. It will be used in live environments where performance, accuracy and usability matter.

About You

You are a true builder who thrives in environments where ownership is high and structure is limited.

You likely bring:

Strong full stack engineering capability with experience shipping products end to end
Hands-on experience applying AI in production, particularly with LLMs, agents or orchestration
The ability to think in systems, not just features
A product mindset with a focus on usability and real-world application
Comfort working directly with complex, imperfect business problem
You may come from: Early-stage or high-growth product companies
A founding or ex-founder background where you’ve built from scratch
Environments where you’ve had to move fast, make decisions and own outcomes
Most importantly, you care about building things that actually get used and deliver impact
Key Benefits

Founding impact – shape the core systems and direction of a new category from day one
Meaningful equity – genuine ownership in an early-stage platform with long-term ambition
High-calibre team – work alongside a small group of top-tier, product-minded engineers
Real-world impact – build systems tied directly to revenue, operations and decision-making
AI-first environment – no legacy constraints, building with modern AI capabilities from the ground up
Complex, interesting problems – operate in real industries where the challenges are nuanced and non-linear
Autonomy and ownership – minimal bureaucracy, high trust and the ability to move quickly
